good.text: You keep ownership and control of your content.
Promises advance notice before terms change.
Tracking is limited or can be opted out of.
Says it does not sell your personal data.

bad.text: The rules can change without telling you.
Your account can be closed at any time, for any reason.
They keep a broad license to content you create.
You are tracked for third-party advertising.

clauses.label: Unilateral Changes
clauses.summary: Beta preview features may be altered or discontinued at any time without any guarantee of support.
clauses.excerpt:
## [K. Beta Previews](#k-beta-previews)

**Short version:** _Beta Previews may not be supported or may change at any time. You may receive confidential information through those programs that must remain confidential while the program is private. We'd love your feedback to make our Beta Previews better._

clauses.label: Unilateral Changes
clauses.summary: GitHub can modify or discontinue beta features at any time without notifying you, and you use them at your own risk.
clauses.excerpt:
## [1\. Subject to Change](#1-subject-to-change)

Beta Previews may not be supported and may be changed at any time without notice. In addition, Beta Previews are not subject to the same security measures and auditing to which the Service has been and is subject. **By using a Beta Preview, you use it at your own risk.**

clauses.label: Unilateral Changes
clauses.summary: Your subscription price stays the same during a payment term, but GitHub can raise prices when the term ends.
clauses.excerpt:
## [1\. Pricing](#1-pricing)

Our pricing and payment terms are available at [github.com/pricing](https://github.com/pricing). If you agree to a subscription price, that will remain your price for the duration of the payment term; however, prices are subject to change at the end of a payment term.

clauses.label: Unilateral Changes
clauses.summary: GitHub may modify or shut down the website, in whole or in part, at any time and without giving notice.
clauses.excerpt:
## [R. Changes to These Terms](#r-changes-to-these-terms)

We reserve the right at any time and from time to time to modify or discontinue, temporarily or permanently, the Website (or any part of it) with or without notice.

clauses.label: Arbitrary Account Termination
clauses.summary: Allows GitHub to immediately terminate accounts of users under 13 or who violate the listed requirements.
clauses.excerpt:
## [3\. Account Requirements](#3-account-requirements)

-   You must be a human to create an Account. Accounts registered by "bots" or other automated methods are not permitted. We do permit machine accounts:
-   A machine account is an Account set up by an individual human who accepts the Terms on behalf of the Account, provides a valid email address, and is responsible for its actions. A machine account is used exclusively for performing automated tasks. Multiple users may direct the actions of a machine account, but the owner of the Account is ultimately responsible for the machine's action

clauses.summary: Users must allow the service and its affiliates to store, copy, analyze, and use their content—including for AI training—without compensation.

You grant GitHub and our Affiliates the right to store, host, archive, parse, display, and make copies of Your Content as necessary to provide, develop, and improve the Service, including by training AI Features, and for the purpose of training, developing, and improving artificial intelligence and machine learning models and technologies of our Affiliates. clauses.label: Broad Content License
clauses.summary: When you add content to a repository with a license notice, you automatically grant the same license to that content and confirm you have the right to do so.
clauses.excerpt:
## [6\. Contributions Under Repository License](#6-contributions-under-repository-license)

Whenever you add Content to a repository containing notice of a license, you license that Content under the same terms, and you agree that you have the right to license that Content under those terms. If you have a separate agreement to license that Content under different terms, such as a contributor license agreement, that agreement will supersede.

clauses.summary: You keep moral rights to your content but waive them so GitHub can use your content without attribution and make adaptations as needed.
clauses.excerpt:

You retain all moral rights to Your Content that you upload, publish, or submit to any part of the Service, including the rights of integrity and attribution. However, you waive these rights and agree not to assert them against us or our Affiliates, to enable GitHub and our Affiliates to reasonably exercise the rights granted in Section D.4, but not otherwise.

clauses.summary: By using automated means to collect public content for commercial AI, you waive any restrictions and allow GitHub to access and use that data without limitation.

By using automated means to access, collect, or otherwise use (“Access”) any publicly accessible Content from the Service for the purpose of developing or training any commercially available artificial intelligence model, machine learning system, or similar technology (a "Commercial AI System"), you hereby waive any and all policies, terms, conditions, or contractual provisions governing products, services, websites or datasets you own or operate that would otherwise prohibit, restrict, or place conditions upon GitHub's Access to any publicly

clauses.label: Arbitrary Account Termination
clauses.summary: GitHub will terminate the accounts of users who repeatedly infringe copyright under its DMCA policy.
clauses.excerpt:

If you believe that content on our website violates your copyright, please contact us in accordance with our [Digital Millennium Copyright Act Policy](/en/site-policy/content-removal-policies/dmca-takedown-policy). If you are a copyright owner and you believe that content on GitHub violates your rights, please contact us via [our convenient DMCA form](https://github.com/contact/dmca) or by emailing [copyright@github.com](mailto:copyright@github.com). clauses.label: Broad Content License
clauses.summary: The service owns all its content and only grants you limited rights to use it in the ways they specify.
clauses.excerpt:
## [G. Intellectual Property Notice](#g-intellectual-property-notice)

**Short version:** _We own the service and all of our content. In order for you to use our content, we give you certain rights to it, but you may only use our content in the way we have allowed._

clauses.label: Broad Content License
clauses.summary: GitHub retains all IP rights to the website and service and restricts you from copying or reusing any design elements without written permission.
clauses.excerpt:
## [1\. GitHub's Rights to Content](#1-githubs-rights-to-content)

GitHub and our licensors, vendors, agents, and/or our content providers retain ownership of all intellectual property rights of any kind related to the Website and Service. We reserve all rights that are not expressly granted to you under this Agreement or by law. The look and feel of the Website and Service is copyright © GitHub, Inc. All rights reserved. You may not duplicate, copy, or reuse any portion of the HTML/CSS, JavaScript, or visual design elements or concepts without express written permission from GitHub.

clauses.label: Broad Content License
clauses.summary: To use GitHub’s trademarks or logos you must follow their trademark guidelines, otherwise you may not use them.
clauses.excerpt:
## [2\. GitHub Trademarks and Logos](#2-github-trademarks-and-logos)

If you’d like to use GitHub’s trademarks, you must follow all of our trademark guidelines, including those on our logos page: [https://github.com/logos](https://github.com/logos).

clauses.label: Arbitrary Account Termination
clauses.summary: GitHub can suspend or permanently block your API access if it deems your usage abusive or excessive, with only a warning email before suspension.
clauses.excerpt:
## [H. API Terms](#h-api-terms)

**Short version:** _You agree to these Terms of Service, plus this Section H, when using any of GitHub's APIs (Application Programming Interface), including use of the API through a third party product that accesses GitHub._

Abuse or excessively frequent requests to GitHub via the API may result in the temporary or permanent suspension of your Account's access to the API. GitHub, in our sole discretion, will determine abuse or excessive usage of the API. We will make a reasonable attempt to warn you via email prior to suspension.

You may not share API tokens

clauses.label: Broad Content License
clauses.summary: Unless you opt out, you grant GitHub and its affiliates a broad license to use your inputs and outputs to train and improve AI models.
clauses.excerpt:

We use your Inputs to generate Outputs and provide the AI Features. You also grant GitHub and its Affiliates a license to collect and use your Inputs and Outputs to develop, train and improve artificial intelligence and machine learning models and technologies including those that power AI Features, unless (a) you opt out through your account settings, or (b) your use of the Service is governed by a GitHub Customer Agreement or volume licensing agreement. clauses.label: Broad Content License
clauses.summary: Any feedback you provide gives GitHub a perpetual, royalty‑free, worldwide license to use and commercialize it.
clauses.excerpt:
## [3\. Feedback](#3-feedback)

We’re always trying to improve our products and services, and your feedback as a Beta Preview user will help us do that. If you choose to give us any ideas, know-how, algorithms, code contributions, suggestions, enhancement requests, recommendations or any other feedback for our products or services (collectively, “Feedback”), you acknowledge and agree that GitHub will have a royalty-free, fully paid-up, worldwide, transferable, sub-licensable, irrevocable and perpetual license to implement, use, modify, commercially exploit and/or incorporate the Feedback into

clauses.label: Arbitrary Account Termination
clauses.summary: GitHub can suspend or end your access to any part of the site at any time, for any reason, without notice.
clauses.excerpt:
## [3\. GitHub May Terminate](#3-github-may-terminate)

GitHub has the right to suspend or terminate your access to all or any part of the Website at any time, with or without cause, with or without notice, effective immediately. GitHub reserves the right to refuse service to anyone for any reason at any time.

clauses.label: Third-Party Tracking
clauses.summary: GitHub uses essential and non‑essential cookies to keep you logged in, remember preferences, secure the service, and for analytics, and also uses cookies on enterprise pages to personalize ads and measure targeted advertising.
clauses.excerpt:
## [Cookies and tracking technologies](#cookies-and-tracking-technologies)

GitHub uses cookies to provide, secure and improve our Service or to develop new features and functionality of our Service. For example, we use them to (i) keep you logged in, (ii) remember your preferences, (iii) identify your device for security and fraud purposes, including as needed to maintain the integrity of our Service, (iv) compile statistical reports, and (v) provide information and insight for future development of GitHub. We provide more information about [cookies on GitHub](/en/site-policy/privacy-policies

clauses.label: Third-Party Tracking
clauses.summary: GitHub embeds a pixel tag in emails to detect opens and capture your IP address, and explains cookie lifetimes while noting you can delete them.
clauses.excerpt:
## [Cookies and tracking technologies](#cookies-and-tracking-technologies)

Our emails to users may contain a pixel tag, which is a small, clear image that can tell us whether or not you have opened an email and what your IP address is. We use this pixel tag to make our email communications more effective and to make sure we are not sending you unwanted email.

clauses.label: Third-Party Tracking
clauses.summary: GitHub uses cookies, web beacons, and other technologies to operate its services and, on enterprise marketing pages, for sales and marketing purposes.
clauses.excerpt:
## [What are cookies and similar technologies?](#what-are-cookies-and-similar-technologies)

We use cookies and similar technologies, such as web beacons, local storage, and mobile analytics, to operate and provide our Services. When visiting Enterprise Marketing Pages, like resources.github.com, these and additional cookies, like advertising IDs, may be used for sales and marketing purposes.

Cookies are small text files stored by your browser on your device. A cookie can later be read when your browser connects to a web server in the same domain that placed the cookie. The text in a cookie c

clauses.label: Third-Party Tracking
clauses.summary: GitHub describes web beacons, third‑party content, mobile analytics identifiers, and flash cookies that enable third parties to collect data and serve ads.
clauses.excerpt:
## [What are cookies and similar technologies?](#what-are-cookies-and-similar-technologies)

Web beacons are electronic images (also called “single-pixel” or “clear GIFs”) that are contained within a website or email. When your browser opens a webpage or email that contains a web beacon, it automatically connects to the web server that hosts the image (typically operated by a third party). This allows that web server to log information about your device and to set and read its own cookies. In the same way, third-party content on our websites (such as embedded videos, plug-ins, or ads) results

clauses.label: Third-Party Tracking
clauses.summary: GitHub and its partners use cookies and similar technologies to track your activity, build profiles, and serve targeted ads, sharing this data with third parties.
clauses.excerpt:
## [How do we and our partners use cookies and similar technologies?](#how-do-we-and-our-partners-use-cookies-and-similar-technologies)

The GitHub Services use cookies and similar technologies for a variety of purposes, including to store your preferences and settings, enable you to sign-in, analyze how our Services perform, track your interaction with the Services, develop inferences, combat fraud, and fulfill other legitimate purposes. Some of these cookies and technologies may be provided by third parties, including service providers and advertising partners. For example, our analytics and

clauses.label: Third-Party Tracking
clauses.summary: GitHub uses required, analytics, social media, and advertising cookies, including third‑party tracking for personalized ads and profiling.
clauses.excerpt:
## [How do we and our partners use cookies and similar technologies?](#how-do-we-and-our-partners-use-cookies-and-similar-technologies)

GitHub uses required cookies to perform essential website functions and to provide the services. For example, cookies are used to log you in, save your language preferences, provide a shopping cart experience, improve performance, route traffic between web servers, detect the size of your screen, determine page load times, improve user experience, and for audience measurement. These cookies are necessary for our websites to work.

Analytics

We allow third pa

clauses.label: Third-Party Tracking
clauses.summary: GitHub and third parties use advertising cookies to track which ads you click and purchases you make, tailoring future ads to your interests.
clauses.excerpt:
## [How do we and our partners use cookies and similar technologies?](#how-do-we-and-our-partners-use-cookies-and-similar-technologies)

In addition, GitHub and third parties use advertising cookies to show you new ads based on ads you've already seen. Cookies also track which ads you click or purchases you make after clicking an ad. This is done both for payment purposes and to show you ads that are more relevant to you. For example, cookies are used to detect when you click an ad and to show you ads based on your social media interests and website browsing history.

clauses.label: You Keep Your Content
clauses.summary: The previous clause does not apply to academic research or to users with fewer than 700 million monthly active users, limiting GitHub’s rights in those cases.
clauses.excerpt:
## [9\. Access Reciprocity](#9-access-reciprocity)

This Section D.9 does not apply to Access solely for the purpose of academic research or if, on the date you Access the Content, the number of monthly active users of the products or services made available by you is less than 700 million in the preceding calendar month. For the purposes of this Section, "you" shall refer to you and any entity that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with you (affiliates).

clauses.label: You Keep Your Content
clauses.summary: GitHub will only access private repository content for security, support, service integrity, legal compliance, or with your consent, treating it as confidential.
clauses.excerpt:
## [E. Private Repositories](#e-private-repositories)

**Short version:** _We treat the content of private repositories as confidential, and we only access it as described in Section E.3 below—for security purposes, to assist the repository owner with a support matter, to maintain the integrity of the Service, to comply with our legal obligations, if we have reason to believe the contents are in violation of the law, or with your consent._

clauses.label: You Keep Your Content
clauses.summary: The agreement itself is released under a Creative Commons Zero license, allowing anyone to reuse the policy text freely.
clauses.excerpt:
## [3\. License to GitHub Policies](#3-license-to-github-policies)

This Agreement is licensed under this [Creative Commons Zero license](https://creativecommons.org/publicdomain/zero/1.0/). For details, see our [site-policy repository](https://github.com/github/site-policy#license).

clauses.label: You Keep Your Content
clauses.summary: GitHub states it does not own the user's input or the AI's output, leaving ownership with the user and placing responsibility on them to respect any third‑party rights.
clauses.excerpt:
## [2\. Ownership](#2-ownership)

GitHub does not claim ownership of your Input or Output.

Output may contain material that resembles code or content in the model's training data or that is subject to third-party copyrights or open source license terms. You are responsible for determining whether your use of Output requires a third-party license and for complying with any such license.

clauses.label: Notice Before Changes
clauses.summary: The provider will give you at least 30 days’ notice of any material changes to the terms, such as price increases, before they take effect.
clauses.excerpt:
## [R. Changes to These Terms](#r-changes-to-these-terms)

**Short version:** _We want our users to be informed of important changes to our terms, but some changes aren't that important — we don't want to bother you every time we fix a typo. So while we may modify this agreement at any time, we will notify users of any material changes and give you time to adjust to them._

We reserve the right, at our sole discretion, to amend these Terms of Service at any time and will update these Terms of Service in the event of any such amendments. We will notify our Users of material changes to this Agre

clauses.label: Notice Before Changes
clauses.summary: GitHub will give at least 30 days’ notice before material changes to the privacy statement, via the website or email.
clauses.excerpt:
## [Changes to Our Privacy Statement](#changes-to-our-privacy-statement)

GitHub may periodically revise this Privacy Statement. If there are material changes to the statement, we will provide at least 30 days prior notice by updating our website or sending an email to your primary email address associated with your GitHub account.

clauses.label: Limited Tracking
clauses.summary: The clause informs users they can disable non‑essential cookies via GitHub’s settings or browser tools, giving them control over tracking.
clauses.excerpt:
## [What are your cookie choices and controls?](#what-are-your-cookie-choices-and-controls)

You have several options to disable non-essential cookies:

1.  **Specifically on GitHub Enterprise Marketing Pages**

Any GitHub page that serves non-essential cookies will have a link in the page’s footer to cookie settings. You can express your preferences at any time by clicking on that link and updating your settings.

Some users will also be able to manage non-essential cookies via a cookie consent banner, including the options to accept, manage, and reject all non-essential cookies.

2.  **Gener

clauses.label: Limited Tracking
clauses.summary: The clause explains how you can limit non‑essential cookies and tracking via browser settings, extensions, Do Not Track signals, and Global Privacy Control, and states GitHub will not sell your data.
clauses.excerpt:
## [What are your cookie choices and controls?](#what-are-your-cookie-choices-and-controls)

-   If your browser sends a [Do Not Track](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Do_Not_Track) (DNT) signal, GitHub will not set non-essential cookies and will not load third party resources which set non-essential cookies.
-   Many browsers provide cookie controls which may limit the types of cookies you encounter online. Check out the documentation for your browser to learn more.
-   If you enable a browser extension designed to block tracking, such as [Privacy Badger](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Privacy_B

clauses.label: No Data Sale
clauses.summary: The clause tells users that the company does not sell or share their personal information with third parties for direct marketing, and provides a way for California residents to request confirmation of this practice.
clauses.excerpt:
## [Shine the Light Act](#shine-the-light-act)

Under California Civil Code section 1798.83, also known as the “Shine the Light” law, California residents who have provided personal information to a business with which the individual has established a business relationship for personal, family, or household purposes (“California Customers”) may request information about whether the business has disclosed personal information to any third parties for the third parties’ direct marketing purposes. Please be aware that we do not disclose personal information to any third parties for their direct m

clauses.label: Limited Tracking
clauses.summary: It informs users that GitHub does not engage in profiling that affects legal decisions and outlines rights to opt out, protecting user privacy.
clauses.excerpt:
## [Colorado/Connecticut/Virginia](#coloradoconnecticutvirginia)

If you live in Colorado, Connecticut, or Virginia you have some additional rights:

-   If we deny your rights request, you have the right to appeal that decision. We will provide you with the necessary information to submit an appeal at that time.
-   You have the right to opt out of profiling in furtherance of decisions that produce legal or similarly significant effects concerning the consumer. GitHub does not engage in such profiling as defined by Colorado law, so there’s no need to opt out.

clauses.label: No Data Sale
clauses.summary: GitHub states it does not sell users' covered information under Nevada law and offers a contact for further questions.
clauses.excerpt:
## [Nevada](#nevada)

We do not sell your covered information, as defined under Chapter 603A of the Nevada Revised Statutes. If you still have questions about your covered information or anything else in our Privacy Statement, please send an email to privacy\[at\]github\[dot\]com.

Taxonomy update: 79 clause(s) re-evaluated under the current scoring rules. The document text did not change.

No changes to user-hostile clauses were found. The review did not turn up any new information that would negatively impact users. All reviewed parts of the document appear to remain the same. Users are not affected by changes to this document.

GitHub's privacy policy allows sharing of personal data with various parties, often without consent, which could lead to user data being used for purposes they may not agree with. The policy also uses various tracking technologies, such as cookies and web beacons, to collect data for advertising and analytics. However, users have some control over their data, as they can disable non-essential cookies and request access, deletion, or correction of their personal information. Additionally, GitHub provides notice of changes to the policy at least 30 days in advance.
Taxonomy update: 79 clause(s) re-evaluated under the current scoring rules. The document text did not change.

The company can change terms with at least 30 days' notice. Users give the service a broad license to use, display, and share their posted content. The service and users can use public repository content without payment. GitHub can use private repository content for AI features unless users opt out. No user-hostile changes were found beyond standard terms.
